subject: Government
aims_and_objectives: define the concepts of Government and show their understanding of its principles, institutions and processes.
recognize their role as an informed citizens and their contributions towards the achievement of national development.
evaluate the successes, failures and problems of governments in West Africa.
analyse the constitutional developments and processes of colonial and post - independent government.
appreciate the role of their country as a member of the international community.

papers.description: Will consist of fifty multiple-choice objective questions drawn from the entire syllabus. Candidates will be required to answer all the questions in 1 hour for 40 marks.
This will be a 2-hour essay type test consisting of two sections, Sections A and B, carrying 60 marks.

sections.instructions: Shall contain five questions out of which candidates shall be required to attempt any two.

sections.section_title: Political and Constitutional Developments in West Africa and International Relations
sections.instructions: Shall contain sets of five questions each; one set for one member country. Each candidate is to answer two questions chosen from the set on the country in which he/she is taking the examination.

section_a_elements_of_government.topic: 1. Meaning and Scope of the Subject Matter - Government
content: Government as an institution of the state
Definition of state, features, structure and functions.
Government as a process or art or governing.
Government as an academic field of study - reasons for studying government.

section_a_elements_of_government.topic: 2. Basic Concepts and Principles of Government
content: Basic concepts - State, Society, Nation, Power, Authority, Legitimacy, Sovereignty, Democracy, Political Culture and Socialization, Communalism, Feudalism and oligarchy, Liberalism, Socialism, Communism, Capitalism, Fascism, Aristocracy, Totalitarianism.
Meaning and features.

Basic principles - Rule of Law, Fundamental Human Rights, Separation of Powers/Checks and Balances, Constitutionalism, Political participation, Representative Government, Centralization and Decentralization - (Delegated Legislation, Devolution and Decentralization).
Definition and Sources
Functions
Types and Features, (Written and Unwritten, Rigid and Flexible).
The Executive, the Legislature and the Judiciary - Judicial Independence. (Types, Structure, Composition/membership; Functions; powers and limitations).

section_a_elements_of_government.topic: 5. State Structure and Characteristics of Government
content: Types - Unitary, Federal, Confederal, Presidential or Non-parliamentary, Parliamentary or Cabinet, Monarchical and Republican.
Meaning, types, features, merits and demerits.
Meaning, mode of acquisition, rights, duties and obligations of individuals in the state.
Means of safeguarding rights of citizens.
Political Parties - definition, organization/structure, and functions.
Party Systems - definition, types, merits and demerits.

section_a_elements_of_government.topic: 8. Pressure Groups, Public Opinion and Mass Media
content: Pressure Groups - definition, types, mode of operation and functions;
Public opinion - definition, formation, Measurement and importance;
Mass Media - definition, roles and impact.

section_a_elements_of_government.topic: 9. The Electoral Systems, Processes and Electoral Management Body
content: Electoral Systems and Processes (Election- Meaning and purpose of elections; Franchise - meaning, and limitations; Types, advantages and disadvantages of Electoral Systems).
Electoral Management Body - definition, function, problems/constrains.
Public/Civil Service - Meaning, Structure, Characteristics and Functions.
Public/Civil Service Commission - meaning and Functions.
Public Corporations - definition, purposes, functions, control, challenges, need for commercialization and privatization.
Local Governments - Meaning, structure, Purposes, Functions, Sources of revenue, control and problems.

section_b_political_and_constitutional_d.topic: 11. Pre-Colonial Political System of Candidates' Respective Countries

content.nigeria: Hausa/Fulani, the Yoruba and the Igbo.

content: The Policy and Structure of the British Colonial Administration - Crown Colony, Protectorate and Indirect Rule.
The Policy of French Colonial Administration - Assimilation and Association (Loi Cadre).
Impact, Advantages and disadvantages of Colonial Rule.
Nationalism: Meaning, factors and effects.
Key Nationalist leaders/movements and their contributions.

section_b_political_and_constitutional_d.topic: 14. Constitutional Developments in Candidates' Respective Countries
content: Pre-Independence Constitutions (Nigeria: Clifford 1922, Richards 1946, Macpherson 1951, Littleton 1954; Ghana: Clifford 1916, Guggisberg 1925, Burns 1946, Arden Clarke 1951, Nkrumah 1954; Sierra Leone: Slatter 1924, Stevenson 1947, Beresford Stooke 1951, The 1956 and 1958 Constitutions; The Gambia)

Post-Independence Constitutions (Nigeria: Independence 1960, Republican 1963, 1979, 1989, 1999; Ghana: Independence 1957, Republican 1960, 2nd Republican 1969, 3rd Republican 1979, 4th Republican 1992; Sierra Leone: Independence 1961, Republican 1971, 1978, 1991; The Gambia: Independence 1965, Republican 1970, 2nd Republican 1997; Liberia: Independence/Republican 1947, 2nd Republican 1985)

section_b_political_and_constitutional_d.topic: 15. Development of Major Political Parties in the Candidates' Respective Countries
content: Formation, Objectives, Sources of Finance, Achievements and Failures for Nigeria, Sierra Leone, The Gambia, and Liberia.

section_b_political_and_constitutional_d.topic: 16. Military Rule in the Candidates' Respective Countries
content: Causes, effects and various military regimes in Nigeria, Ghana, Sierra Leone, The Gambia, and Liberia.

section_b_political_and_constitutional_d.topic: 17. Federal/Unitary Systems of Government in West Africa
content: Origin, Factors, Structure, Features and Problems in Nigeria, Sierra Leone, The Gambia, and Liberia.

section_b_political_and_constitutional_d.topic: 18. Foreign Policies of the Candidates' Respective Countries
content: Definitions, Factors, objectives, advantages and disadvantages for Nigeria, Ghana, Sierra Leone, The Gambia, and Liberia.

The United Nations Organizations (UNO), The Commonwealth of Nations, African Union (AU) - NEPAD, The Economic Community of West African States (ECOWAS). Origin, aims/objectives, achievements and problems.

suggested_reading_list.author: Adu Boahen, A
suggested_reading_list.title: Ghana Evolution and Change in the 19th and 20th Centuries

suggested_reading_list.author: Garbrah . K.
suggested_reading_list.title: Foreign Policies of Major Countries, Subject Publications, New Delhi

suggested_reading_list.author: Amoa G. Y.
suggested_reading_list.title: Groundwork of Government in West Africa

suggested_reading_list.author: Dare and Oyewole
suggested_reading_list.title: A Textbook of Government for Senior Secondary School
